trends.name: Global Economy in Transition Game
trends.description: Global economy navigating a transition phase rather than clean recovery or recession — with multiple scenario branches (baseline, negative, positive) reflecting genuine uncertainty in macro trajectory.

signals.name: Real Estate Relegated from Investment Premier League
signals.description: Commercial real estate losing its premium investment status in 2024 — rate sensitivity, occupancy shifts, and value corrections demoting the asset class from institutional portfolio priorities.

signals.name: Dollar Winning Streak Ending
signals.description: US dollar's multi-year strengthening trend forecast to reverse in 2024 as rate differentials narrow and global growth rebalances — with significant implications for EM assets and global trade.

scenarios.name: Beautiful Game Scenario
scenarios.description: Helaba's 10% probability positive scenario where global growth surprises upward, inflation normalizes, and financial conditions ease — setting the stage for broad risk asset appreciation.

trends.name: Niche Culture Acceleration in Luxury
trends.description: Luxury consumption fragmenting into accelerating niche subcultures where consumer identity drives brand value — displacing the traditional aspirational-mass model that defined legacy luxury.

trends.name: Consumer-Led Luxury Redefinition
trends.description: Luxury evolving from brand-dictated prestige to consumer-defined value — with new codes, new creators, and new inclusion paradigms rewriting the rules established over decades.

signals.name: New Luxury Codes Replacing Heritage Signals
signals.description: Traditional luxury status signals (heritage, craftsmanship claims, exclusivity) being supplemented or replaced by new codes — community belonging, creator provenance, and cultural participation.

signals.name: Collaboration as Luxury Innovation Engine
signals.description: Brand collaboration emerging as the primary luxury innovation mechanism — with unexpected partnerships generating cultural relevance that traditional brand-alone campaigns cannot achieve.

trends.name: Vacation Maximization as Travel Mindset
trends.description: Travelers in 2025 adopting a maximization mindset — combining relaxation with high-impact adventures to extract maximum value from both time and financial investment in travel.

trends.name: Travel Companion Diversity — Frolleagues and MeMooners
trends.description: New travel companion configurations emerging — workplace friends ('Frolleagues') and solo travelers seeking self-connection ('MeMooners') — reshaping hospitality product and experience design.

signals.name: Hurkle-Durkling as Intentional Rest Travel
signals.description: Deliberate rest and purposeful inactivity ('hurkle-durkling') becoming a recognized travel category — counter-programming to the maximization trend and reflecting wellbeing-oriented travel demand.

trends.name: Identity as Central Media Theme
trends.description: Cultural identity — personal, communal, and brand — emerging as the organizing theme for media strategy in 2024, shaping everything from AI personalization to ecommerce emotional experience.

trends.name: Emotional Ecommerce Experience
trends.description: Digital commerce evolving beyond transactional utility toward emotionally resonant experiences — with identity, discovery, and human connection becoming core ecommerce design requirements.

signals.name: Cookie Loss Triggering Identity Infrastructure Rebuild
signals.description: Third-party cookie deprecation forcing a fundamental rebuild of digital identity and targeting infrastructure — with first-party data, contextual, and AI-based approaches filling the gap.

trends.name: National Capability to Leverage Megatrends
trends.description: Countries diverging in their ability to convert global megatrends into national opportunity — with governance, infrastructure, and institutional quality determining who captures transformation benefits.

signals.name: Future Possibilities Index Measuring Country Readiness
signals.description: Emergence of quantitative indices measuring countries' readiness to harness megatrends — providing governments and investors with structured foresight on national transformation potential.

trends.name: Engineered Friendship — Designed Social Connection
trends.description: As organic social infrastructure weakens, intentionally engineered social experiences — run clubs, brand communities, gamified connection — filling the gap that institutions and algorithms have vacated.

trends.name: Efficiency Mode as Consumer Value Shift
trends.description: Consumers and brands shifting to efficiency-first orientation — extracting maximum value from existing resources rather than seeking novelty — reflecting both economic pressure and decision fatigue.

signals.name: Gaming Reality Blending Physical and Digital
signals.description: Gaming mechanics, aesthetics, and social structures permeating physical reality and non-gaming contexts — with brands and media companies deploying game logic to drive engagement.

signals.name: Skilled Labor Renaissance
signals.description: Growing recognition and demand for skilled trade labor as economic and cultural re-evaluation of manual expertise accelerates — with implications for education, brand positioning, and workforce design.

trends.name: Attitudinal Brand Voice Revival
trends.description: Brands returning to distinct, opinionated, attitude-driven communication after years of purpose-washing homogeneity — differentiation through voice, perspective, and cultural specificity.

trends.name: Product as Firmware — Continuous Update Model
trends.description: Consumers increasingly valuing products that evolve and update post-purchase (firmware model) over static objects — with brands that enable continuous product development building stronger loyalty.

signals.name: Run Clubs as Social Infrastructure
signals.description: Running clubs becoming high-trust social ecosystems functionally analogous to institutional social structures — combining fitness, identity, and community in ways that brands are beginning to sponsor and emulate.

signals.name: Tennis Cultural Ascent
signals.description: Tennis expanding beyond its traditional demographic and cultural positioning — becoming a broader cultural touchpoint with growing brand and media investment following its mainstream moment.

trends.name: New Growth Engines Replacing Post-Pandemic Drivers
trends.description: Investment landscape shifting toward identification of new structural growth engines as pandemic-era tailwinds exhaust — with AI, climate action, and emerging market recovery as candidate themes.

signals.name: Climate Action as Investment Theme Maturing
signals.description: Climate-related investment themes maturing from speculative to institutional — with HSBC private banking identifying climate action as a structural growth engine alongside AI and emerging markets.

trends.name: Creator Economy Dominance of Social
trends.description: Social media ecosystem fundamentally organized around creator primacy — with brands increasingly subordinating themselves to creator relationships to access authentic audience engagement.

trends.name: Social Media as Primary Research Channel
trends.description: Consumers using social media as their primary research and discovery engine — for products, services, and information — displacing traditional search and brand-owned channels.

signals.name: AI Tools Mainstream in Social Content Creation
signals.description: Social media marketers broadly adopting AI tools for content creation and optimization — with AI moving from experimental to standard workflow component in social strategy.

signals.name: Social Commerce Fully Embraced by Consumers
signals.description: In-app social commerce completing its mainstream transition — with consumers comfortable completing purchases within social platforms, no longer requiring redirect to external retail sites.

trends.name: Sales Rep Evolving to Consultant
trends.description: The informed, digitally-empowered buyer shifting the sales function from persuasion to consultation — with buyers arriving further along the purchase journey requiring sellers to add genuine expertise value.

trends.name: AI Automating Sales Administrative Work
trends.description: AI tools enabling sales professionals to reclaim time from administrative tasks — with sellers spending only 33% of time selling, AI rebalancing toward customer-facing activity.

signals.name: Self-Service Sales Growing in B2B
signals.description: B2B buyers increasingly preferring self-service purchase paths for lower-complexity decisions — requiring sales organizations to provide digital-first buying experiences alongside human relationships.
